Wilo Blue destination

Story

Discover the tale of Wilo, his ocean, and the friends he meets along the way.

Locations

The World of Wilo Blue

The Murmuring Reef
A tangled coral maze that whispers with every tide. Its currents speak not in words, but in memories and feelings.
The Breathless Pit
A trench so deep, even light refuses to follow. Ancient. Cold. Endless. Most who descend never return.
The Black Bloom
A field of black kelp that moves without current, as if alive and waiting. The place where Rose vanished.
The Drowned Vault
An ancient sunken structure guarded by three legendary Guardians who test the heart, the self, and the future.
The Power Plant
A single luminous plant rooted deep in the vault. Delicate, ancient, alive and capable of granting the power to walk between worlds.
The Surface
Where the ocean meets the sky. Warm sunlight on new skin. The world above, vast, strange, and full of things with no name yet.
A small soul in a vast ocean.
The Story

A Journey Born from Loss

Wilo wakes in the endless blue with only fragments of memory and a feeling he cannot name. The ocean is beautiful, ancient, and alive with secrets, but every glow around him hints at a hidden ocean power waking up inside him.

MemoryFragments return through songs, lights, and places Wilo somehow recognizes.
FriendshipLost companions help him turn fear into courage beneath the waves.
DestinyThe deeper he travels, the closer he gets to the truth behind Orkanis.
The ocean does not give Wilo answers. It gives him signs.
Chapter OneThe first light appears in the silence.
Chapter TwoA friend arrives where fear should be.
Chapter ThreeA shadow moves behind the current.
View Pitch Package

Story World

Wilo Blue begins with a clear emotional hook: a small hero lost in a vast ocean, searching for home while discovering courage, friendship, and hidden mythology.

Action
The Drowned Vault

The Three Guardians

Ancient beings who guard the path to the Power Plant. Each tests a different truth what you have carried, who you truly are, and what you are willing to become.

Thalrax
The Armored One
First Guardian. Stone and lava forged into ancient form he tests what you have carried and what you have endured.
Mirael
The Veiled One
Second Guardian. A mirror made living she shows you not who you pretend to be, but who you truly are.
Kuroth
The Many-Eyed One
Third Guardian. Hundreds of eyes, each seeing a different moment in time. He asks: if power changes you, will you still be you?

Wilo Blue is a fantasy adventure anime about courage, friendship, and the question every lost soul must answer: what does it mean to find home?

Did You Know?

Secrets of the Whale

Whales are among the most mysterious creatures on Earth. Dive into their world and discover what makes them extraordinary.

Interactive

Which Character Are You?

The Creator

Behind Wilo Blue

Nagi Alaaeldin
Creator of Wilo Blue
Egypt
@nagui.alaaeldin
Story CreatorWorld BuilderOcean Voice

Wilo Blue was born from a simple but powerful feeling the feeling of being swept away from everything you know, and having to find your own way back.

I wanted to create a story that speaks to every child and every adult who has ever felt small in a big world. The ocean became the perfect universe for that journey vast, mysterious, beautiful and dangerous all at once. Just like life.

But Wilo Blue is also a love letter to the ocean itself. To the whales. To every creature that calls the deep blue home. Because their world is real and it needs us.

"Every great journey begins with a current that pulls you away from shore. Trust it. It knows where you need to go."

Nagi Alaaeldin

Creative Core

A small hero in a huge world.

The story begins with Wilo feeling lost, then expands into a cinematic ocean mythology about courage, friendship, and home.

Visual Identity

Blue mystery, light, and wonder.

The world is designed for animated spectacle: glowing reefs, ancient guardians, deep-sea shadows, and emotional ocean magic.

Purpose

Entertainment with an ocean heart.

Wilo Blue is built to move audiences while quietly inspiring them to care about whales and the real ocean.

Collaboration & Studio Portal

Connect with the world of Wilo Blue. Whether you are an animation studio, a creative collaborator, or a deep-sea advocate, let's explore possible paths together.

Or reach out directly: nagialaaeldin.04.28@gmail.com +966 56 310 7546
+

Our Voice

Why the Ocean Matters

"

The ocean covers 71% of our planet. It breathes life into every living thing on Earth. It regulates our climate, feeds billions of people, and holds more wonder than we have ever discovered. And right now it is crying for help.

"

The Whale

Whales are not just animals. They are the engineers of our ocean. A single whale can sequester as much carbon as thousands of trees. When they dive deep and surface, they bring nutrients that feed the entire ocean food chain. Without whales, the ocean dies. Without the ocean, we die.

The Ocean

The ocean produces over 50% of the oxygen we breathe. It absorbs 30% of the carbon dioxide humans produce. It is the lungs of our planet. Yet every minute, a truckload of plastic enters the sea. Every year, millions of marine animals die from pollution, fishing nets and habitat loss. The time to act is now.

'

Be Part of the Story

Wilo found his way home because he never stopped swimming. The ocean gave him friends, wisdom and a second chance. Now it is our turn to give something back. Share this page. Talk about ocean conservation. Make one small change today. Because every ripple matters.

Take the Pledge Follow @wilo.blue
Depth
0m
Interactive

Dive Into the Deep

The ocean has five zones. Each one darker, stranger and more mysterious than the last. Scroll down to dive.

SCROLL TO DIVE
0 " 200 metres

The Sunlight Zone

The ocean's surface layer, flooded with sunlight. This is where almost all ocean life begins. Photosynthesis happens here, fish schools shimmer, dolphins leap, and whales raise their calves in warm, lit waters.

Temperature: 17 degreesC " 28 degreesC. Light: Full sunlight. Pressure: Normal. Life: Abundant " over 90% of all marine species live here.

Whales Dolphins Sharks Coral Reefs
📖 DIVE LOG BOOK
0%

Track your coordinates down to the ocean floor. Scroll deep into each of the five marine layers to automatically complete your descent card.

1. Sunlight Zone
0 - 200m
2. Twilight Zone
200 - 1000m
3. Midnight Zone
1000 - 4000m
4. Abyssal Zone
4000 - 6000m
5. Hadal Zone
6000 - 11000m
Wilo's World
This is where Wilo was born and where he first met Rose. The warm currents, coral gardens, and dancing sunbeams near the surface that Hakmoon described.
'
Did You Know?
The sunlight zone produces over 50% of Earth's oxygen " more than all the rainforests combined. Every second breath you take comes from here.
The Murmuring Reef
This is where Wilo, Rose and Oli heard whispers in the currents. The twilight zone is where sounds travel strangely and light plays tricks on the mind.
Bioluminescence Begins
At around 600m, creatures begin glowing with their own light. 76% of deep sea animals produce bioluminescence " the ocean's living stars.
200 " 1,000 metres

The Twilight Zone

Sunlight fades to a faint blue glow. No photosynthesis can happen here. Pressure builds. Temperatures plunge. And yet, this zone teems with life " mysterious, adapted, glowing in the dark.

Temperature: 4 degreesC " 17 degreesC. Light: Dim blue twilight. Pressure: 20"100x surface. The daily migration: millions of creatures swim up to feed at night and retreat here by day.

' Giant Squid Jellyfish Octopus Lanternfish
1,000 " 4,000 metres

The Midnight Zone

Total darkness. Not a single photon of sunlight reaches here. The pressure is 400 times what you feel on the surface. And yet creatures thrive, glowing with their own cold light, hunting in silence.

Temperature: 2 degreesC " 4 degreesC. Light: Absolute darkness. Pressure: 400x surface. Snow: Constant "marine snow" " particles of dead organisms drift down like snowfall.

Anglerfish Vampire Squid Viperfish Bioluminescence
-
The Black Bloom
When Orkanis took Rose into the darkness, this was his domain. The midnight zone " where silence becomes weight and light becomes a weapon.
Marine Snow
A constant gentle fall of organic particles drifts down through the midnight zone. It is the primary food source for creatures that never see the sun.
"
Eye of the Abyss
The chamber where Orkanis held Rose. The abyssal zone " vast, flat, featureless except for hydrothermal vents that crack the seafloor with superheated water and minerals.
Hydrothermal Vents
Cracks in the ocean floor vent water at 400 degreesC. Yet entire ecosystems thrive around them " tube worms, blind shrimp and bacteria that feed on chemicals instead of sunlight.
4,000 " 6,000 metres

The Abyssal Zone

The vast, flat plains of the deep ocean floor. Covering over 60% of Earth's surface, it is the largest habitat on the planet, and one of the least explored. More humans have walked on the moon than have visited the deepest parts of this zone.

Temperature: near 0 degreesC. Light: None. Pressure: 600x surface. Floor: Soft sediment up to 500m thick, containing millions of years of marine snow.

Sea Cucumbers Blind Crabs Snailfish Vent Life
6,000 " 11,000 metres

The Hadal Zone

Named after Hades, the Greek underworld. The deepest ocean trenches on Earth. The Mariana Trench reaches 11,034 metres " if you placed Mount Everest at the bottom, its peak would still be 2km underwater.

Temperature: 1 degreesC " 4 degreesC. Light: None. Pressure: 1,000x surface " enough to crush a submarine. Despite this: life exists. Microbes, snailfish and amphipods thrive in the deepest places on Earth.

Amphipods Hadal Snailfish Microbes Trenches
The Power Plant
In the world of Wilo, something ancient and alive grows at the very bottom of everything. Where no light reaches, where pressure crushes stone. That is where true power waits.
Beyond Space
We have better maps of Mars than we do of the deep ocean floor. Less than 25% of the ocean has been mapped. The hadal zone is Earth's last true frontier.
You have reached the bottom
Now rise with Wilo
' Surface
Awareness

Ocean Health Meter

The ocean is in crisis. These are the real numbers " sourced from leading scientific organisations and research institutions.

" All figures are published estimates from NOAA, IUCN, IPCC, and peer-reviewed science. Not real-time data.
Ocean Health Indicators
Based on IPCC 2023, IUCN, and NOAA published assessments
' What You Can Do
" Sources
Our Mission

Save the Ocean

Wilo Blue is more than a movie. It is a reminder of what we stand to lose. The ocean is in danger and every one of us can help.

10,000
Blue Whales Left
The blue whale is the largest animal on Earth and it is critically endangered
8M
Tons of Plastic Per Year
Every year 8 million metric tons of plastic enter our oceans, harming marine life
50%
Coral Reefs Lost
Half of the world coral reefs have died in the last 30 years due to warming oceans
1,000+
Species Threatened
Over 1,000 marine species are currently threatened with extinction

What You Can Do

Refuse Single-Use Plastic
Say no to plastic bags, straws and bottles
-
Join Beach Cleanups
Volunteer at local ocean and beach cleanup events
"
Spread Awareness
Share this page and talk about ocean conservation
'
Support Organizations
Donate or volunteer with ocean conservation groups
Adopt a Whale
Symbolically adopt a real whale and fund research
Choose Sustainable Food
Pick sustainably sourced seafood to protect marine ecosystems
